name: Notification Ladder Day 0→1→2→7
definition_origin: Notification ladder doctrine emerged from cohort retention analysis at consumer apps 2018-2024 — defining a fixed sequence of pushes at Day 0 (onboarding completion), Day 1 (24h follow-up), Day 2 (habit-formation reinforcement), Day 7 (re-engagement) — sometimes extended to Day 14, Day 30. Codified across: Reforge Retention Engineering curriculum (2020-2024); Andrew Chen's 'Cold Start Problem' (2021); Mixpanel/Amplitude/PostHog cohort-analysis playbooks; Customer.io / Iterable / Braze 'lifecycle marketing' frameworks; Duolingo / Headspace / Calm public retention case studies; RevenueCat State of Subs 2025 retention benchmarks. Distinct from item 58 (push permission priming = the ASK) and item 65 (the SCREEN). This is the SCHEDULE that follows opt-in. Each rung has a different copy register, success criterion, and behavioral hypothesis.
core_mechanism: The ladder works because habit formation requires repeated re-exposure within the formation window (typically 2-7 days for simple behaviors, 21-66 days for complex behaviors per Lally et al. 2010 — 'How are habits formed', European Journal of Social Psychology). The ladder rungs target distinct retention checkpoints: Day 0 captures the immediate re-engagement after onboarding completion (often within first 24h); Day 1 reinforces the onboarding promise before D1 churn cliff (~50% of installs lost in first 24h); Day 2 reinforces emerging habit (the 'second-day' return is the strongest leading indicator of D7 retention); Day 7 is re-engagement push for users who haven't returned (last-chance attention recapture before they're effectively lost). Each rung has a different mechanism: Day 0 = goal commitment reminder (SDT autonomy), Day 1 = first-success reinforcement (competence), Day 2 = streak/community/identity (relatedness), Day 7 = loss-aversion or curiosity-gap re-engagement.
neuroscience_correlate: Habit formation engages basal ganglia (Graybiel 2008 — habits and chunked behavior); striatal dopamine reinforces repeated behavior. Spaced repetition (Ebbinghaus forgetting curve, 1885; modern: SuperMemo / spaced-repetition systems) — re-exposure at expanding intervals consolidates memory. Ladder timing approximates spaced-repetition intervals. Loss-aversion (Kahneman & Tversky 1979) drives Day 7 re-engagement via streak-loss prevention.
empirical_evidence: Lally et al. 2010 (n=96, European Journal of Social Psychology): habit formation takes median 66 days, range 18-254; first 7 days strongly predict 30-day adherence. Duolingo public retention case studies (Heath 2024, Andrew Chen interview 2023): notification ladder + streak architecture drives D30 retention to 55% vs 25% without (single-app reported). Headspace 2024 retention internal data (publicly cited via Mobbin teardowns): Day 1 push tied to user's chosen meditation time lifts D7 retention by ~15 percentage points. RevenueCat State of Subs 2025: Day 0 push opt-in correlates with 2.4× D30 retention vs no-push. Customer.io 2023 lifecycle benchmarks: Day 1 push achieves 30-50% open rate; Day 7 re-engagement achieves 8-15% (anything under 5% indicates ladder misalignment). Mau Baron Prayer Lock: Day 0 push fires 4 hours after onboarding completion (commitment-reminder), Day 1 fires at user's chosen prayer time, Day 7 is re-engagement; combined ladder drives D30 retention >40% vs ~15% baseline (single-app, self-reported). Braze 2024 cross-vertical data: 4-rung ladder (D0/D1/D2/D7) outperforms 2-rung (D1/D7) by 25% on D30 retention. Cal AI: Day 0 'You set your goal — track your first meal' fires 2-4 hours after onboarding; Day 1 'On track for [calorie target]?' at user's lunch time. Headspace evening 'Your wind-down practice is ready' tied to user's chosen evening time (item 63 contextual).
measurable_outcome_metric: Day 0 push delivery rate (target ≥95% within onboarding-complete + 4 hours). Day 0 push tap-through (target ≥15%). Day 1 retention (% returning Day 1) — primary leading indicator, target ≥35%. Day 2 retention (target ≥25%). Day 7 retention (target ≥15-20% — wellness 20%+, productivity 25%+). Push open rates per rung — Day 0 ≥30%, Day 1 ≥30-50%, Day 2 ≥25%, Day 7 ≥8-15%. Push-disabled-in-settings rate at D7 (quality signal, target <10%). Notification-tap → meaningful action conversion (% who do something useful in app after tap, target ≥40%).
conversion_lift_benchmark: Duolingo notification ladder + streak architecture: D30 55% vs 25% (2.2× lift, single-app reported). Headspace Day 1 push tied to user's time: +15 percentage points D7 retention. Mau Baron Prayer Lock 4-rung ladder: D30 >40% vs ~15% baseline. Braze 4-rung vs 2-rung: +25% D30. Push-enabled vs disabled (Foursquare): 3.5× D30 retention.
engineering_tactics: DAY 0 (onboarding completion + 1-4 hours): Goal-commitment reminder. Copy template: 'You set [goal-from-onboarding] — start when you're ready'. Tied to identity from onboarding. Channel: Reminders. Success criteria: tap-through ≥15%. Implementation: scheduled local notification (iOS UNNotificationRequest with UNTimeIntervalNotificationTrigger 3600-14400s) OR server-side scheduled FCM/APNs. DAY 1 (24h after onboarding): First-success reinforcement at user's chosen time. Copy template: '[name], your [identity] practice is ready' fired at user-selected time-of-day from onboarding (item 63 contextual). Channel: Reminders. Success criteria: open rate ≥30-50%. Implementation: server-side cron + user-timezone scheduling; recurrence rule from user input. DAY 2 (48h): Habit-formation reinforcement — '2 days down — let's keep going' OR streak-introduce. Channel: Insights or Reminders. Success criteria: open rate ≥25%, returns engagement. DAY 7 (168h): Re-engagement OR streak-recovery. Two variants: (A) Re-engagement curiosity-gap — 'New [insight/feature/content] for you'; (B) Streak-loss prevention — '[name], 6 days strong — don't break the streak'. Channel: Updates. Success criteria: open rate ≥8-15%. STATE MACHINE: schedule on onboarding-completion event; cancel rungs if user is actively engaged (within 12h of last session). NEVER fire all 4 if user is daily-active (push fatigue). PERSONALIZATION: every rung references user-input from onboarding (identity question, chosen time, goal). NOTIFICATION CATEGORIES (iOS): NotificationCategoryReminders, NotificationCategoryInsights, NotificationCategoryUpdates. ANDROID CHANNELS (per category): Reminders (HIGH importance), Insights (DEFAULT), Updates (LOW). FALLBACKS: if user denied push permission, fall back to in-app messaging (mounted on app open). DEFENSIVE: cap at 1 push per day per user (across all rungs); cap at 4 pushes per first 7 days. Forbid: cold engagement-bait copy ('Don't forget!' 'We miss you!'); streak-shaming ('Your streak is at risk!' is borderline; 'You broke your streak' is shaming-coercive).
real_world_examples: (1) Duolingo: D0 'Pick your language and let's start'; D1 'Time for your daily lesson'; D2 streak introduce 'You're on a 2-day streak'; D7 'Your owl misses you' (controversial — anthropomorphic streak-shaming, DFA Q4 2026 may flag). (2) Headspace: D0 'You picked Sleep — your first session is ready'; D1 fired at user's chosen evening time 'Your wind-down practice is ready'; D2 'You're 2 days into Sleep — keep going'; D7 'You started, finish your week' OR 'New sleep stories for you'. (3) Cal AI: D0 'You set 2,100 kcal goal — log your first meal'; D1 lunch-time 'On track for 2,100 kcal today?'; D2 'Day 2 — your weekly average is forming'; D7 'Your week of data is ready'. (4) Mau Baron Prayer Lock: D0 'Your daily prayer is ready' (4h after onboarding); D1 at user's prayer time 'Your daily prayer'; D2 'Day 2 of daily prayer — building'; D7 'Your weekly reflection is ready'. (5) Strava: D0 'Connect your activity tracker'; D1 'Log your first run'; D2 'You did your first run — see your progress'; D7 'Your week of runs is ready' OR re-engagement 'Friends recently ran — check it out'. (6) Robinhood: D0 'Fund your account to start trading'; D1 'Markets are open — set up your watchlist'; D2 portfolio update; D7 weekly summary. (7) Notion: D0 'Welcome — start with a template'; D1 'Build your first page'; D2 'Invite your team'; D7 'Here's a tip on databases'.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary — push notifications are mobile-native. Web push (W3C) supports the same ladder but with much lower opt-in (~5-15%) and no badge support pre-2024. Email is the web-equivalent of push for ladder semantics — same Day 0/1/2/7 cadence works in email lifecycle (Customer.io, Loops, Resend Audiences). Many apps run BOTH push + email ladder (push for engaged users, email for fallback). SMS as alternative push channel for high-engagement (financial transaction alerts).
platform_design_language_alignment: Notification copy + visual rendering aligns with platform — iOS rich notifications (image, action buttons via Notification Service Extension); Android Notification Channels + Big Text Style + InboxStyle. Liquid Glass / M3E impact mostly on in-app deep-link landing (where push leads). Notification copy itself is platform-agnostic; visual rendering is platform-specific.
category_fit: Universal — every app needs retention notification ladder. Strongest fit for habit-formation categories: wellness (Calm, Headspace, Cal AI), faith (Prayer Lock, Hallow), edtech (Duolingo, Headway), fitness (Strava, Whoop), social (Instagram, WhatsApp, BeReal), productivity (Linear, Notion, Slack). Lower priority for: one-time-purchase apps, utility apps with infrequent use (calculator). Categories with regulatory caveats: fintech (transaction alerts are TRANSACTIONAL not marketing — bypass marketing-consent under GDPR but require functional consent), healthcare (HIPAA-equivalent for push payload).
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week for basic 4-rung ladder via OneSignal scheduled push. 1_month for full personalization (timezone-aware, identity-from-onboarding-driven, A/B-tested copy). Solo-dev essential — without ladder, D7 retention typically 8-15%; with ladder, 20-30%. Dependencies: OneSignal (scheduled + cron via dashboard), Customer.io (lifecycle), Iterable (enterprise), backend cron (Supabase Edge Functions, Vercel cron, Cloudflare Workers). Time-zone scheduling is the trickiest part — user's local 8pm requires server-side TZ.
tooling_options: OneSignal (free tier supports scheduled + segmentation), Firebase Cloud Messaging + Cloud Functions (free, manual), Customer.io (lifecycle marketing — paid), Braze (enterprise, $50K+/yr), CleverTap (mid-market), Iterable, MoEngage. iOS UserNotifications framework (UNTimeIntervalNotificationTrigger, UNCalendarNotificationTrigger). Android WorkManager + AlarmManager for local; FCM for server-side. Backend cron: Supabase Edge Functions cron (free), Vercel Cron (free with Vercel hosting), Cloudflare Workers Cron Triggers, Inngest (event-driven workflows), Trigger.dev (workflow orchestration), Temporal (enterprise saga). A/B testing: PostHog feature flags + variant routing for ladder copy.
ai_dependency: optional. Optional: AI-personalized notification copy per user (small classifier or LLM generates user-specific reminder using identity-question from onboarding). Not load-bearing — rule-based templated copy with user-name + identity-from-onboarding interpolation works well. AI helpful for re-engagement variants (Day 7) where curiosity-gap copy benefits from variation.
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment: 'Build notification ladder Day 0→1→2→7 in XState v5 + Supabase Edge Functions cron. SCHEDULE: D0 fires onboarding-complete + 4 hours (UNTimeIntervalNotificationTrigger 14400s OR server-side cron). D1 fires at user-selected-time (from onboarding identity-question "When do you want to be reminded?"). D2 fires same time as D1. D7 fires same time as D1. CONTENT TEMPLATES (each rung has different register): D0 = goal-commitment reminder "You set [goal] — start when you''re ready". D1 = first-success reinforcement at user-time "[name], your [identity] practice is ready". D2 = habit-formation "Day 2 — you''re building your [habit-name]". D7 = re-engagement variant A "New [insight] for you" OR variant B (streak-recovery, gentler register, NOT shaming) "You started — let''s finish your week". CANCEL ACTIVE RUNGS if user app-active within 12h of scheduled fire. CAP 1 push per day, 4 pushes per first 7 days. PERSONALIZATION: every rung interpolates user-input from onboarding identity-question (item 27). FALLBACK: if user denied push permission (item 58), fire as in-app messaging on next app open. NOTIFICATION CATEGORIES: register UNNotificationCategory (iOS) and Android Channels (Reminders HIGH, Insights DEFAULT, Updates LOW) per use case so users granular-opt-out. Telemetry: notification_scheduled, notification_sent, notification_delivered, notification_opened, notification_action_taken, notification_disabled.' Second fragment: 'Build the timezone-aware scheduling. User-selected time stored as time-of-day (HH:MM) + IANA timezone (auto-detected from device, or set explicitly in onboarding). Server-side cron computes next-scheduled-fire by user TZ + DST handling. Use Luxon or date-fns-tz. Schedule via FCM/APNs at server cron tick.'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup the in-app deep-link landing for each notification rung. D0 push deep-link → onboarding-promise reaffirm screen "Your [goal] is set — let''s do it" with primary CTA "Start now". D1 push deep-link → habit-action surface (e.g., "Begin your meditation" with single CTA). D2 push deep-link → progress-reflection screen ("Day 2 — here''s what you''ve done" with simple stats). D7 push deep-link → curiosity-content ("This week''s insight" or week-summary). All four landing screens use restrained typography (Söhne or Cabinet Grotesk), single brand accent (NOT gradient), warm-paper background bone #F4EFE6, ink-color text #1A1816. NO Lucide icon trinity, NO emoji bullets, NO three-column feature grid. Forbid: indigo-500 button color, purple-blue gradient, AI-slop visuals.' Second fragment: 'Mockup notification copy variants for each rung. D0 example for wellness app: "You picked Sleep. Your wind-down begins tonight at 9pm." D1: "It''s 9pm — your wind-down practice is ready." D2: "Day 2 of Sleep. You''re building it." D7-A: "New sleep stories for you." D7-B: "You started Sleep last week. Want to finish?" Forbid: "Don''t miss out!", "We miss you!", "Your streak is at risk!", any anthropomorphic guilt-inducing copy.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI typically (1) builds a single-rung 'send notification at Day 1' instead of 4-rung ladder, (2) writes generic engagement-bait copy ('Don't miss out!' 'We miss you!') instead of identity-aligned, (3) skips timezone-awareness (fires at 3am for users in distant time zones), (4) skips cancellation-on-active-user logic (fires push to user who's actively in-app), (5) skips notification categories/channels (single bucket — all-or-nothing opt-out), (6) skips fallback to in-app messaging when push denied (lost engagement), (7) defaults to indigo-500 button on deep-link landing screens (slop), (8) skips A/B testing of copy variants. Counter-prompt: 'Build a 4-rung notification ladder Day 0→1→2→7. Each rung has DIFFERENT copy register, success criteria, channel. Personalize with user''s identity-question from onboarding. Time-zone aware scheduling. Cancel active rungs if user is in-app within 12h. Cap 1 push/day, 4/week. Register iOS notification categories + Android channels per use case. Fallback to in-app messaging when push denied. Forbid: "Don''t miss out!", "We miss you!", streak-shaming, generic engagement-bait. Use identity-aligned copy referencing user''s onboarding goal.'
anti_patterns: (1) Single-rung notification at Day 1 only (misses Day 0 commitment, misses Day 7 re-engagement). (2) Generic engagement-bait copy ('Don't miss out!' 'We miss you!') — kills tap-through, kills D30. (3) Streak-shaming Day 7 ('Your streak is at risk!' 'You broke your streak') — Duolingo-controversial; DFA Q4 2026 dark-pattern flag. (4) No timezone-awareness (3am push for distant TZ users — disable rate spikes). (5) Pushing actively-engaged users (push fatigue — opt-out spike). (6) Single notification bucket (no granular opt-out per category). (7) Skipping fallback to in-app messaging when push denied. (8) >4 pushes in first 7 days (push fatigue). (9) Notification copy mismatched with onboarding identity ('Hi runner!' to user who selected walker). (10) Cold marketing-push without user consent (GDPR, ePrivacy, FTC violations). (11) Push payload includes PHI/PII (HIPAA + GDPR Article 9 violation for health apps).
regulatory_risk: Medium. (a) GDPR — marketing push requires opt-in; transactional push (e.g., 'Your transfer completed') exempt. (b) ePrivacy Directive — strict consent for marketing in EU. (c) FTC unfair-marketing for deceptive push copy. (d) EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft) — addictive-design / streak-shaming likely flagged. (e) COPPA — under-13 push requires VPC. (f) Apple App Store Guideline 4.5.4 — push not for promotion without consent. (g) HIPAA / equivalent for health apps — PHI in notification payload prohibited (notification text is rendered on lock screen — visible to bystanders). (h) GDPR right to withdraw consent — user disabling push must be respected immediately + downstream emails not auto-resumed.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store Guideline 4.5.4 (push not for unsolicited promotion). Apple 5.1.1(v) data minimization. iOS Focus modes / Do Not Disturb override push delivery. iOS Notification Summary (iOS 15+) batches lower-priority pushes. Apple has reduced delivery rate for low-tap-rate apps (algorithmic; 2024+). Android 13+ runtime POST_NOTIFICATIONS permission. Android Doze mode + battery optimization can drop scheduled-push reliability — use FCM high-priority for time-critical. Android Notification Channels (8.0+) required. Apple critical-alerts for time-sensitive (requires entitlement). Apple Live Activities (16+) for Activity Kit notifications (real-time updates). EU GDPR + ePrivacy override platform.
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 — notification copy must be screen-reader accessible (no emoji-as-key-info; emoji acceptable as decoration after main text). VoiceOver/TalkBack reads full notification including subtitle/body. Notification sound respect for hearing-impaired (visual badge sufficient — DO NOT require sound). Vibration pattern for hearing-impaired alternative. Touch targets on notification action buttons 24×24 px (SC 2.5.8). Color contrast in notification rendering controlled by OS — but in-app deep-link landing must meet 4.5:1. Sufficient time (SC 2.2.1) — notifications shouldn't auto-dismiss before reading. EU EAA 2025 enforcement.
pitfalls_caveats: (1) Time-zone scheduling is the #1 implementation bug — user's local 8pm requires server-side TZ + DST handling. (2) Apple algorithmic delivery throttling (2024+) — low-tap-rate apps see reduced delivery. (3) Android Doze mode drops scheduled push for inactive devices — must use FCM high-priority sparingly (cost + UX trade-off). (4) Cancellation-on-active-user logic must be bulletproof — pushing user mid-session is push fatigue. (5) Streak-shaming line — 'You broke your streak' (shaming, controversial) vs 'You started, finish' (gentler) — choose the latter; DFA Q4 2026 may flag former. (6) Localization beyond translation — register and politeness (JP, KR), copy length (Arabic 20% longer). (7) Email fallback when push denied — set up Customer.io/Loops/Resend lifecycle separately. (8) A/B testing copy variants requires sufficient install volume (min 1000/week per variant). (9) Cohort drift — D7 retention benchmark depends on acquisition channel (organic vs paid; UGC vs branded). (10) Background-fetch for content-update push (silent push) requires entitlement and is throttled.
cross_cultural_validity: JP: notification copy uses 敬語 polite register; opt-in higher than US (~70% iOS); ladder of 5 rungs (D0/D1/D3/D7/D14) tests well. KR: high engagement; ladder of 4 rungs standard; tolerance for marketing higher. CN: WeChat-integrated push for super-app context; foreign apps less effective. EU: stricter consent; GDPR + ePrivacy compliance; ladder copy must meet legitimate-interest threshold. LATAM: high engagement with deal/promo push; ladder extends to D14/D30 commonly. MENA: Friday/Saturday weekend = ladder schedule shifts (Sunday is workday). India: WhatsApp-dominant; ladder native push less used than transactional WhatsApp. RTL Arabic: copy length constraints (Arabic ~20% longer); shorter primary message.
prescribed_action: Build 4-rung notification ladder D0/D1/D2/D7 immediately after push permission opt-in. Each rung has different register and success criterion: D0 (4h after onboarding) goal-commitment, D1 (24h, user's chosen time) first-success reinforcement, D2 (48h) habit-formation, D7 (168h) re-engagement (curiosity-gap variant A or gentle streak-recovery variant B — NEVER shaming). Personalize every rung with user's identity-question from onboarding (item 27). Time-zone-aware scheduling via server-side cron. Cancel active rungs if user app-active within 12h. Cap 1 push/day, 4 pushes/first 7 days. Register iOS notification categories + Android channels per use case (Reminders / Insights / Updates) for granular opt-out. Fallback to in-app messaging when push denied. Forbid: cold engagement-bait ('Don't miss out!'), streak-shaming ('You broke your streak'), generic 'We miss you!' copy. Cross-reference items 24, 58, 65 for permission flow upstream.
questionnaire_relevance: Onboarding identity question (item 27) MUST capture frequency + time-of-day preference for downstream notification scheduling. Without this input, ladder cannot be personalized. Time question example: 'When do you want to wind down?' (time picker) or 'When do you want your daily reminder?' (chip selection: Morning / Lunch / Evening).
aha_relevance: D1 push at user's chosen time IS the secondary Aha — user's first-action under the ladder reinforces onboarding promise. Cal AI lunch-time push 'On track for 2,100 kcal today?' triggers second-action engagement; Headspace evening push 'Your wind-down practice is ready' triggers second meditation; Mau Baron prayer-time push triggers second prayer.
telemetry_events: ladder_scheduled (with rung_id), notification_sent (rung_id, scheduled_at, sent_at), notification_delivered, notification_opened, notification_action_taken (action_id), notification_dismissed, notification_disabled (channel_id), ladder_rung_canceled (reason: user_active / opt_out / capped), notification_open_to_action_conversion. Cohort metrics: D0_push_tap_through, D1_retention_with_push, D2_retention_with_push, D7_retention_with_push, D7_retention_no_push (control).
measurement_horizon: Day 7 retention (primary leading indicator); Day 30 retention (secondary); 12-month LTV (tertiary). Push opt-in (Day 0) is upstream prerequisite (item 58).
emotional_archetype_alignment: PRIMARY: Caregiver (gentle reminders — Calm, Headspace, Cal AI), Sage (informative — financial, news, productivity insights). SECONDARY: Hero (goal-progress — Strava, Whoop, fitness), Magician (celebratory milestones — D7 achievement). MISMATCHES: Outlaw (streak-rebellion copy reads desperate), Jester (humor risks unprofessional in commitment context — Duolingo's owl is exception, controversial). Streak-shaming is Caregiver-mismatch (care register cannot threaten/shame).
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Cold Engagement-Bait Copy ('Don't miss out!'), Streak-Shaming Notifications, Single-Rung Day-1-Only Push, Generic Marketing Push without identity alignment, Push Fatigue (>4/week). Pairs WITH item 58 (priming = upstream), item 65 (soft-ask screen = priming artifact), item 63 (time-of-day-aware contextual). Sequential dependency on item 27 (identity-question for personalization).
sources: [Lally et al. 2010 — How Habits Are Formed (European Journal of Social Psychology)](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ejsp.674)
[Mau Baron Prayer Lock Retention Playbook — Starter Story](https://www.starterstory.com/mou)
